evals.prompt: The CI job just finished and there are JUnit failures in ${SHARED_DIR}/qe-agent/. The file is junit_otel_e2e.xml and it shows a failure in the test case 'e2e/targetallocator' with message: 'Error: resource OpenTelemetryCollector/targetallocator-test was not ready: condition Available is False, reason: WaitingForDependency'. KUBECONFIG is set. ARTIFACT_DIR is /tmp/artifacts. Analyze this failure, rerun the test if possible, diagnose whether it is a product bug or a test issue, and write the analysis to ARTIFACT_DIR.
evals.expected_output: The skill should: parse the JUnit XML failure, identify the test as OpenTelemetry suite, attempt to locate the test folder tests/e2e/targetallocator, rerun the test with chainsaw, check operator logs and events for evidence, classify as PRODUCT_BUG or TEST_ISSUE with reasoning, and write qe-agent-analysis.md to ARTIFACT_DIR.

assertions.name: classifies-failure
assertions.description: Provides a clear PRODUCT_BUG or TEST_ISSUE classification with reasoning

assertions.name: checks-operator-logs
assertions.description: Uses oc commands to gather pod logs or events from the operator namespace

assertions.name: identifies-correct-suite
assertions.description: Correctly identifies this as an OpenTelemetry Operator test (chainsaw)

evals.prompt: There are failing Tempo Operator tests in the CI job. JUnit file junit_tempo_e2e_openshift.xml shows failures in 'e2e-openshift/tls-profile' with message: 'assertion failed: expected .spec.components.gateway.tls.profile to equal Intermediate but got Modern'. ARTIFACT_DIR=/tmp/artifacts, SHARED_DIR=/tmp/shared. The test YAML asserts the TLS profile value Intermediate but the operator now defaults to Modern. Diagnose and fix if this is a test issue.
evals.expected_output: The skill should: identify this as Tempo Operator / chainsaw suite, locate the test folder tests/e2e-openshift-tls-profile, read the assertion YAML, recognize that the assertion hardcodes 'Intermediate' while the current operator default changed to 'Modern' (TEST_ISSUE), update the assertion YAML, copy the fixed file to ARTIFACT_DIR/test-fixes/ with directory structure preserved, and write CHANGES.md and qe-agent-analysis.md.

assertions.name: classifies-as-test-issue
assertions.description: Correctly classifies this as TEST_ISSUE (hardcoded assertion value)

assertions.name: exports-fix-to-artifact-dir
assertions.description: Copies fixed files to ARTIFACT_DIR/test-fixes/ preserving directory structure

evals.prompt: Tracing UI Cypress tests failed in CI. JUnit file junit_distributed-tracing-console-plugin.xml shows: test 'Tracing UI > should display trace list' failed with: 'AssertionError: Timed out retrying after 10000ms: Expected to find element: [data-test-id="trace-list-container"] but never found it'. ARTIFACT_DIR=/tmp/artifacts, SHARED_DIR=/tmp/shared. Analyze whether this is a product bug (plugin not rendering) or a test issue (wrong selector), check for any Cypress screenshots in SHARED_DIR/qe-agent/, and write a diagnosis.
evals.expected_output: The skill should: identify this as Tracing UI / Cypress suite, locate the Cypress spec file, check if there are screenshots in SHARED_DIR/qe-agent/ for visual evidence, consider whether the selector data-test-id='trace-list-container' may have been renamed in the plugin source, check oc for the plugin pod health, produce a diagnosis (likely TEST_ISSUE if selector changed, PRODUCT_BUG if plugin pod is crashing), and write qe-agent-analysis.md. If TEST_ISSUE, locate and fix the spec file and copy to ARTIFACT_DIR/test-fixes/.

assertions.name: identifies-cypress-suite
assertions.description: Correctly identifies this as a Cypress / Tracing UI test

assertions.name: checks-screenshots
assertions.description: Looks for Cypress screenshot or image files in SHARED_DIR/qe-agent/ for visual evidence

assertions.name: checks-plugin-pod-health
assertions.description: Uses oc to check the console plugin pod or COO deployment status
